title: Why Quantum Computing Isn’t a Serious Risk for Bitcoin Yet: CoinShares
why:
- Quantum computing is frequently cited as a potential future threat to Bitcoin due to its ability to break current cryptographic algorithms.
- Experts indicate that quantum computers currently do not have enough qubits, error correction, and stability to threaten Bitcoin in practice.
- The transition to quantum-resistant cryptography for Bitcoin is seen as achievable well before quantum computers become advanced enough to present a real risk.
- Current discussion suggests quantum technology is still several years away from having practical implications for Bitcoin security.
